title: Zeltnera exaltata
intro: Zeltnera exaltata, also known as tall blue-cup or tall blue-loosestrife, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Zeltnera exaltata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Zeltnera exaltata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to support digestive and immune system health.
taxonomy: Zeltnera exaltata is a species within the plant family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the gentian or snapdragon family. This family is part of the larger clade of flowering plants, known as angiosperms. The classification of Zeltnera exaltata can be further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. Its order is Gentianales, and it is a member of the family Gentianaceae. Overall, Zeltnera exaltata's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other flowering plants in the Gentianaceae family.
uses: Zeltnera exaltata, also known as tall blue-cup, is a plant with a rich history in traditional medicine. Its leaves and stems have been used by some indigenous cultures to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Additionally, it has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential as a medicinal resource.

The plant's properties may also aid in reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds.
